But when I use the same management app shell in the application and set the isActive properties based on the URL, I don't see them visually activated.

Can you investigate what is going on or what is going wrong? Thank you. @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/management-app-shell.tsx @Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/layouts/AuthenticatedAppShell.tsx @Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/layouts/AuthenticatedAppShell.tsx:82 @Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/layouts/AuthenticatedAppShell.tsx:74

Tracing how `isActive` flows from the app into the management shell and comparing it to Storybook. Reading the shell components and `AuthenticatedAppShell`.

input.query: Where is PanelsLayout defined and how does it render children?

content.text:
Identifying the issue: Radix `Slot` merges `SidebarMenuButton` props (including `data-active` and `className`) onto the direct child `SidebarNavLink` via `cloneElement`. `SidebarNavLink` only forwards `to`, `className`, `onClick`, and `aria-current` to `Link`/`<a>`, so `data-active` and other merged props never reach the DOM. Active styles use `data-active:bg-sidebar-accent` on the interactive element, so they never apply. Storybook can look correct for collapsible parents because their trigger uses `SidebarMenuButton` without `asChild` (see `CollapsibleTrigger`), so `data-active` stays on the real `<button>`.

[REDACTED]

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Cause

`SidebarMenuButton` uses Radix **`Slot`** with `asChild`. That merges the button props (including **`data-active`** and the sidebar **`className`** with `data-active:…` utilities) onto its **single child** via `cloneElement`.

The child is **`SidebarNavLink`**, which then rendered `react-router-dom` **`Link`** (or `<a>`) but only passed **`to`**, **`className`**, **`onClick`**, and **`aria-current`**. Everything else the `Slot` merged in—especially **`data-active`**—never reached the real `<a>`.

Active styling in `sidebar.tsx` depends on the **anchor** carrying `data-active` (e.g. `data-active:bg-sidebar-accent`). Without it on the DOM node, the item never looks active.

Your **`isNavItemActive` + `pathname` logic is fine** (we also verified `matchPath` for `/dashboard`, `/requests`, etc.).

## Why Storybook looked fine

In `app-shell.stories.tsx`, many highlighted examples are **collapsible group headers** (`CollapsibleTrigger` → `SidebarMenuButton` **without** `asChild`). There the `data-active` stays on a real **`<button>`**, so the active look works even though the same bug exists on **`asChild`** rows (plain primary links and sub-links under `SidebarNavLink`).

## Fix

`SidebarNavLink` is now a **`forwardRef`** wrapper that spreads **`...rest`** (and **`ref`**) onto **`Link`** / **`<a>`**, so `Slot`-merged props like **`data-active`**, **`data-slot`**, **`data-sidebar`**, etc. reach the element that actually gets the Tailwind `data-active:` rules.

Change is in ```153:196:/Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx```.

After pulling this in, sidebar items in `AuthenticatedAppShell` should show the same active styling as in Storybook when `isActive` is true.

[REDACTED]
